qualitative_claims.claim: First formally verified anomaly constraint in particle physics (abstract, conclusions)
qualitative_claims.support: All referenced Lean modules exist, have zero sorry (for Paper 9's modules), lake build clean. Comprehensive formal verification of Z16 anomaly from fermion data.
qualitative_claims.notes: Priority/novelty claim. Cannot verify 'first' against entire literature but the scope of verification (19+23+13+9 theorems across 4 modules) is substantial and credible.

qualitative_claims.claim: First Drinfeld center computations in a proof assistant (abstract, conclusions)
qualitative_claims.support: ToricCodeCenter.lean (25 thms), S3CenterAnyons.lean (22 thms), CenterEquivalenceZ2.lean (10 thms), VecGMonoidal.lean (12 thms). All zero sorry.
qualitative_claims.notes: Priority/novelty claim. Credible given extensive Lean formalization.

qualitative_claims.claim: Three generations without nu_R require hidden sectors (line 99)
qualitative_claims.support: Lean theorem hidden_sector_required in Z16AnomalyComputation.lean proves: if a != 0 in ZMod 16, hidden sector needed. Three-gen anomaly = -3 mod 16 != 0.

qualitative_claims.claim: Braided monoidal structure requires G commutative (CommGroup G), line 169
qualitative_claims.support: VecGMonoidal.lean uses CommGroup G section. Lean typeclass synthesis confirms requirement.
qualitative_claims.notes: Correct -- Mathlib Center braiding requires commutativity for Day convolution symmetry.

project_level_counts.location: Abstract line 33: '936 theorems and 2 axioms across 63 Lean 4 modules'
project_level_counts.notes: Verified: grep '^theorem' across lean/SKEFTHawking/*.lean = 936. grep '^axiom' = 2 (GaugeErasure.lean, GoltermanShamir.lean). 63 .lean files.

project_level_counts.location: Abstract line 33-34: 'with 251 filled by the Aristotle automated prover'
project_level_counts.notes: ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS in constants.py has 254 entries (254 total = 251 machine + 3 manual). The paper says '251' which happens to match the machine-only count but is inconsistent with the conclusions (line 276) which says '254'. Should pick one consistent number. Recommend '254 tracked (251 automated, 3 manual)' or just '251 automated'.

project_level_counts.location: Conclusions line 275: 'Verified by lake build (zero sorry)'
project_level_counts.notes: ModularInvarianceConstraint.lean has 4 theorems with sorry (zeta24_pow_24, zeta24_ne_one, zeta24_primitive, qParam_integer_invariant) plus 2 sorry branches in framing_anomaly_constraint. The project is NOT zero-sorry. The abstract (line 32) scopes correctly to 'All modules referenced in this paper' which is true (Paper 9 modules are all zero-sorry), but the conclusions claim global zero-sorry which is false.

project_level_counts.location: Conclusions line 276: '254 theorems are filled by the Aristotle automated prover'
project_level_counts.notes: 254 includes 3 manual entries. '251 automated + 3 manual' would be precise. Inconsistent with abstract's '251'.

project_level_counts.notes: Verified: non_abelian_center_discrete in GaugeErasure.lean, gs_nogo_axiom in GoltermanShamir.lean.
